name: Valle de Ojén Cyclist Trail
name_original: Carril cicloturista Camino de Ojén - Parque Natural de Los Alcornocales – circular desde Facinas
description: This difficult 48.6-mile touring bicycle route through Los Alcornocales Natural Park offers cork oak forests and mountain views from Facinas
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tarifa offers diverse landscapes where the Mediterranean Sea meets the Atlantic Ocean. The region features a nearly 40-kilometer coastline with pristine beaches, golden dunes, tranquil coves, and rocky cliffs. Inland, the terrain transitions to lush, forest-covered mountain ranges, including the significant cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park. This varied topography provides routes ranging from coastal paths to challenging mountain and gravel trails. Best touring cycling routes around Tarifa The most popular touring cycling route is Valle de Ojén Cyclist Trail, a 48.6 miles (78.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route immerses cyclists in lush vegetation and cork oak forests within Los Alcornocales Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Natural Park of the Strait by EuroVelo 8 – circular from Tarifa, a difficult 45.0 miles (72.3 km) path. This challenging route traverses fine sand beaches, rocky areas, and cliffs within the unique Estrecho Natural Park. Local touring cyclists also love the Tarifa Point – Tarifa Point loop from Casas de Porro, a 16.1 miles (25.8 km) trail leading through coastal views and dunes, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Tarifa is defined by diverse terrain, coastal paths, and cork oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain trails. The routes in Tarifa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 290 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tarifa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with nearly 100 routes documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 20 easy, 23 moderate, and 49 difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa's touring cycling routes feature incredibly diverse terrain. You can explore nearly 40 kilometers of coastline with pristine beaches, golden dunes, tranquil coves, and rocky cliffs. Inland, the landscape transitions to lush, forest-covered mountain ranges, including the significant cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park. This varied topography provides options from coastal paths to challenging mountain and gravel trails.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Tarifa?
A: Yes, Tarifa offers several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968570874/natural-park-of-the-strait-by-eurovelo-8-circular-from-tarifa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Strait by EuroVelo 8 – circular from Tarifa&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 72.3 km path through coastal landscapes. Another option is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarifa Point – Tarifa Point loop from Casas de Porro&lt;/a&gt;, covering 25.8 km with coastal views.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints accessible by touring bike in Tarifa?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Tarifa offer stunning panoramic views. You can find exceptional vistas of the Strait of Gibraltar, where Africa is visible, from points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Tarifa – southernmost point of continental Europe&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/838636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Tarifa from the Dunes – Gravel route loop from Tarifa&lt;/a&gt; also provides great perspectives.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy touring cycling routes in Tarifa?
A: Yes, Tarifa has 20 easy touring cycling routes suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarifa Point – Tarifa Point loop from Casas de Porro&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25.8 km trail that leads through coastal views and dunes.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit along touring cycling routes in Tarifa?
A: Beyond natural beauty, touring cyclists can encounter several historical and cultural sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/255197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guzmán el Bueno Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/370731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Castle&lt;/a&gt; in Tarifa. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21824836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarifa Old Town loop from Tarifa&lt;/a&gt; route takes you through the charming Old Town with its narrow cobblestone streets.

Q: What natural parks can I explore by touring bike in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa is home to two significant natural parks ideal for touring cycling. The &lt;strong&gt;Estrecho Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, a critical point for migratory birds, features fine sand beaches, rocky areas, and cliffs, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968570874/natural-park-of-the-strait-by-eurovelo-8-circular-from-tarifa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Strait by EuroVelo 8 – circular from Tarifa&lt;/a&gt; route. The &lt;strong&gt;Los Alcornocales Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its cork oak forests, offers lush vegetation and wildlife spotting opportunities, which you can experience on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976158570/valle-de-ojen-cyclist-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Ojén Cyclist Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Tarifa?
A: The touring cycling experience in Tarifa is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 290 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal paths with views of the Atlantic and Mediterranean to the lush cork oak forests inland. The variety of routes, catering to all ability levels, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes that pass through Tarifa?
A: Yes, Tarifa is part of the EuroVelo 8 route, a long-distance cycling path that follows the Atlantic coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968570874/natural-park-of-the-strait-by-eurovelo-8-circular-from-tarifa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Strait by EuroVelo 8 – circular from Tarifa&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that incorporates this renowned European network, offering fantastic views and connecting to areas like Gibraltar and Algeciras.

Q: What unique natural features can I see while touring cycling in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa boasts several unique natural features. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/370696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolonia Dune&lt;/a&gt;, a striking natural monument, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6194993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meeting Point of the Atlantic Ocean and Mediterranean Sea&lt;/a&gt;. The region's nearly 40-kilometer coastline also offers pristine beaches and rocky cliffs, providing a distinct cycling backdrop.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa offers a unique cycling experience year-round. While its consistent winds are famous for water sports, they also contribute to exhilarating rides. The region's mild climate generally makes it suitable for touring cycling outside of the peak summer heat, with spring and autumn offering particularly pleasant conditions for exploring its diverse landscapes.
tips: This road is part of the Eurovelo 8 route, its 16 kilometers are of total peace and tranquility.
It is an excellent place surrounded by amazing nature, its cork oaks and its tracks make cycling very enjoyable.

faqs.question: How difficult is this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This cycling route is categorized as difficult, requiring a very good fitness level. While generally navigable, its length of approximately 78.1 km and an elevation gain of 975 meters contribute to its challenge. Some unpaved segments may also require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mixed terrain. You'll encounter approximately 22.7 km of asphalt and 13.8 km of paved surfaces. However, be prepared for significant unpaved sections, including about 31.8 km of compacted gravel and nearly 10 km of other unpaved surfaces. The path includes roads, streets, and some dedicated paths, traversing varied topography with mountains, valleys, and rivers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: It is estimated to take around 5 hours and 33 minutes to complete this cycling route. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What natural features and sights can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the stunning Los Alcornocales Natural Park, renowned for its vast cork oak forests. The route offers views of these magnificent cork forests, rugged mountains, and diverse natural landscapes. The Ojén valley section, particularly the CA221 road, is especially attractive with its cork forests and verdant river woodland. You might also catch panoramic views of the Strait of Gibraltar and the African coastline from certain points.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits needed for cycling in Los Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling on established routes within Los Alcornocales Natural Park does not require a specific permit. However, it's always advisable to check the official park regulations for any updates or specific restrictions, especially concerning off-trail cycling or protected areas. There are no entrance fees for the park itself.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential heavy rains in winter. This allows for a more comfortable and enjoyable experience through the park's diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route in Facinas?
faqs.answer: When starting this circular route from Facinas Village, you can typically find parking within the village itself. As a small town, there are usually public parking areas or street parking available, though it's recommended to arrive early, especially on busy days, to secure a spot close to your starting point.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Los Alcornocales Natural Park, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the mixed terrain and potential for encountering other cyclists or vehicles. Be prepared for the route's length and difficulty, and consider if it's suitable for your dog's endurance.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, a part of it follows the Ojén Trail, which is also part of the Eurovelo 8 route. You will also encounter segments of the Andalucía (Province of Cádiz) trail, Transandalus, and the Corredor Verde Dos Bahías.

faqs.question: What makes Los Alcornocales Natural Park unique?
faqs.answer: Los Alcornocales Natural Park is unique for several reasons. It boasts one of the largest and best-preserved cork oak forests in the world, making it a significant center for cork production. It's also a declared Biosphere Reserve and part of the Mediterranean intercontinental biosphere reserve. The park is home to rare relict laurel forests (laurisilva), a subtropical forest type found in very few other places in continental Europe, contributing to its exceptional biodiversity.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Facinas Village, where you can find cafes and places to resupply before and after your ride. However, given the length of the route and its passage through a natural park, options for food and water directly along the trail may be limited. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration.
